steps.title: Spot the signal of refusal
steps.content: The claim requires a quotation showing Henry NOT providing comfort. The strongest signal is “sometimes choose to absolutely repudiate” paired with “bully”—explicit words showing Henry refusing to comfort Jimmie and doing the opposite instead.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content: C shows “Henry would sometimes choose to absolutely repudiate [Jimmie's behavior]” and “bully him most virtuously”—illustrating refusal and criticism instead of comfort. B shows Henry usually providing comfort (contradicting the claim), A addresses what impresses Jimmie rather than his treatment, and D focuses on Henry's guilt rather than his response to Jimmie's shame. That matches choice C — C is correct.
